option.name: -a
tip: Number of KDF rounds when deriving a new key from a passphrase.

option.name: -A
tip: Generate all default host key types (dsa, ecdsa, ed25519, rsa).

option.name: -b
tip: Number of bits in the key to create (default 3072 for RSA, 256 for ECDSA/ED25519).

option.name: -B
tip: Show the bubblebabble digest of a key.

option.name: -c
tip: Change the comment in the private and public key files.

option.name: -D
tip: Download the public keys provided by a PKCS#11 token.

option.name: -e
tip: Export a private or public key to a file.

option.name: -E
tip: Hash algorithm used when displaying fingerprints.

option.name: -f
tip: Specify the filename of the key file.

option.name: -F
tip: Search a known_hosts file for a host.

option.name: -g
tip: Use generic DNS format when printing the resource record.

option.name: -h
tip: When signing a key, create a host certificate instead of a user certificate.

option.name: -i
tip: Read an unencrypted private (or public) key file and print a public key to stdout.

option.name: -I
tip: Identity (certificate). A unique identifier that is logged by the server.

option.name: -k
tip: Generate a KRL (Key Revocation List) file.

option.name: -K
tip: Download host keys from a KDC (Key Distribution Center).

option.name: -l
tip: Show the fingerprint of a public key file.

option.name: -L
tip: Print the contents of a certificate.

option.name: -m
tip: Specify the key format when importing or exporting keys.

next.name: screen
tip: Screen candidate DH primes for safety.

option.name: -n
tip: Specify principals (user or host names) for a certificate.

option.name: -O
tip: Specify a key or certificate option.

option.name: -p
tip: Change the passphrase of a private key file.

option.name: -Q
tip: Test whether a KRL is signed by the specified CA.

option.name: -R
tip: Remove all keys belonging to a hostname from a known_hosts file.

option.name: -s
tip: CA key for signing or verifying.

option.name: -t
tip: Specify the type of key to create.

option.name: -U
tip: Indicate that a CA key is to be used to sign host certificates instead of user certificates.

option.name: -V
tip: Specify a validity interval for a certificate.

option.name: -w
tip: Specify a provider for key operations.

option.name: -y
tip: Read a private key file and print the corresponding public key to stdout.

next.name: find-principals
tip: Find the principal(s) in a signature.

next.name: match-principals
tip: Find allowed principals for a signer identity.

next.name: sign
tip: Sign a file using an SSH signature.

option.name: -z
tip: Specify a serial number for a certificate.

option.name: -Z
tip: Specify the cipher to use for encrypting private keys.
